properties.remark: ICE JAM UPSTREAM FROM THE FLEUR DRIVE GAUGE CAUSING WATER TO RISE RAPIDLY IMPACTING GEORGE FLAGG PARKWAY AND SW 30TH NEAR THE RACCOON RIVER.

FLASH FLOOD DUE TO ICE JAM ALONG THE DES MOINES RIVER NEAR FORT DODGE. TWO TO THREE FEET OF FLOWING WATER THROUGHOUT THE BREEN ADDITION JUST NORTH OF FORT DODGE...WITH SEVERAL HOUSES RECEIVING WATER UP TO THE FIRST PANEL OF GARAGE DOORS.
THE EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS THAT WATER WAS RISING RAPIDLY IN THE RIVERDALE AREA WITH LARGE CHUNKS OF ICE MOVING. WATER WAS FLOWING RAPIDLY. FLOODING CONTINUES IN THE BREEN ARE AS WELL.
FLASH FLOOD DUE TO ICE JAM ALONG SCENIC DRIVE NEAR DES MOINES RIVER WEST BANK. REPORTED BY WEBSTER COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT.

WATER CONTINUES TO RISE RAPIDLY DUE TO AN ICE JAM ON THE WEST FORK OF THE DES MOINES RIVER SOUTH OF ESTHERVILLE. THE PARKLAND AREAS NEAR THE RIVER IN ESTHERVILLE ARE FLOODED. LOW LYING AREAS NEAR THE RIVER ARE ALSO FLOODED DUE TO A SECOND ICE JAM 2 MILES NORTH OF ESTHERVILLE.
